IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-430 Racine, WI** is running hot. Microsoft Mount Pleasant is pulling four crews — Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, Kelso Burnett — all at **$62.51/hr**, the board's ceiling. Long sleeves and safety toes are mandatory, but the work's locked in and the pay's real. Book's thin (76 hands across 11 calls), so you move fast here or you're waiting.

**LU-237 Niagara Falls, NY** has Lake Mariner Data Center eating hours. Ferguson and O'Connell combined are posting 86 Inside JW spots at **$48.20/hr** on first shift 7/10s, per diem available, with rotation orientations Mon-Tue-Thu and Mondays only respectively. Amazon's running a second line at the same rate, 5/10s and 8s. This is volume play — 104 hands on the board but work's steady.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** pays **$44.80/hr** base but the real money's in the structure: Parsons Electric (all three Polaris Forge Data Center postings) stacks **$100/day non-taxed per diem** plus **$100 weekday and $150 weekend incentives** on top. Night shift adds 31.4% differential. You're looking at north of $58/hr effective when the incentives roll. Hunt Electric's at the Air Base same rate, same per diem.

**LU-494 Milwaukee, WI** — Port Washington Data Center jobs are REPOST status across MJ, Gurtz, and Uihlein Electric: **JW wage plus $10/hr** ($10 over scale assumed). That's 38 calls, 182 hands, 6am-7:30am starts. MJ's Job#041 and Gurtz's Job#136 have cycled twice — contractor's keeping seats warm.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** has scale work starting at **$40/hr** (QTS Fayette, Wellstar Paulding) but Miller Electrical's cable splicer role pays **$40.90/hr** with the hard ask: 3 years documented. Inglett & Stubbs runs 4-10s + optional Saturday.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** — Rosendin's overscale play at **$40/hr plus $15 incentive** on 90% of 60-hour weeks (Texas State JIW) undercuts the **$55/hr** NM-licensed roles from Summit, Cupertino, and B&D, but it's work.

Data center builds are consolidating hands: 857 calls chasing 3,324 bodies means your leverage's tight, but regional per diem and shift differentials are where the real money lives — not base wage.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Tuesday, July 7, 2026**

**LU-1547 Anchorage, AK** is the board to watch — 37 calls chasing 63 hands means real traction. Fullford Electric dropped a fresh Inside JW call today at **$57.44/hr** for Anchorage work. Alaska Communications is running **$45.31/hr** Other work, and Golden Valley Electric Association has an 8-week inventory gig in Healy at **$30.86/hr**. The spread is wild, but the volume is there if you can handle the cold.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** stays solid with 10 calls and 92 hands on deck. Summit Line and Cupertino Electric are both pushing **$55/hr** Other work with all OT double time — you'll need an NM State JIW License EE98J to qualify. Rosendin's running the lower track at **$40/hr** with a **$15 incentive** if you're willing to chase 90% of 60-hour weeks. B & D Industries wants fire alarm/VESDA experience for their **$55** slot.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is drowning in calls — 26 openings for 102 hands. Parsons Electric owns the board with three separate Inside JW calls on the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood, all at **$44.80/hr** plus **$100/day non-taxed per diem** (one bumps to **$150 weekends**). Hunt Electric's running 10 JWs at the Grand Forks Air Base, same rate. ND License and drug test required across the board. Book depth is real but the work is real too.

**LU-291 Boise, ID** has 11 calls stacked on 45 hands — Prime Controls dominates with **$170/day per diem x7** if you're over 75 miles out on the Micron ID1 gig. Inside JW pays **$45.33/hr** base plus **$20/hr** on top. Quality Electric's St. Luke's Tower job runs **$55.33/hr** for shop work. PLA/LARP wages lock you in but the numbers pencil.

**LU-1393 Indianapolis, IN** isn't flashy but ARC American's Journeyman Lineman call in Wanatah hits **$64.66/hr** for 34kv and 12.5kv rebuilds with potential OT — book depth sits at 32 hands chasing 25 calls.

Data center builds and utility infrastructure rebuilds are the gravity right now — Polaris Forge, Micron, and Google West Memphis are pulling hands nationwide.
